title: podman secret ls --format fails with listed fields in the documentation
body:
### Issue Description

the --format flag for the `podman secret ls` command fails with some of the fields in listed in the documentation (https://docs.podman.io/en/v5.4.0/markdown/podman-secret-ls.1.html). The doc says the following fields are allowed in the format flag. This is inline with what I get with the tab completion.
```
.CreatedAt
.ID
.SecretData
.Spec
.Spec.Driver
.Spec.Driver.Name
.Spec.Driver.Options
.Spec.Labels
.Spec.Name
.UpdatedAt
```

However, using anything other than  `ID, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, Driver, Name(which is not even in the list but still works)` fails with the error message `Error: template: ls:1:18: executing "ls" at <.Spec.Name>: can't evaluate field Spec in type *types.SecretListReport`. 

 


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Just issue command `podman secret ls --format {{.Spec.Labels}}`  or use any other field from the tab completion that is not one of `ID, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt, Driver, Name`

```
[root@podman ~]#  podman secret ls  --format {{.Spec.Labels}}
Error: template: ls:1:18: executing "ls" at <.Spec.Name>: can't evaluate field Spec in type *types.SecretListReport

[root@podman ~]#  podman secret ls  --format {{.Spec.Name}}
Error: template: ls:1:18: executing "ls" at <.Spec.Name>: can't evaluate field Spec in type *types.SecretListReport

[root@podman ~]# podman secret ls  --format {{.Name}}
my_secret_test
my_secret
```

### Describe the results you received

command fails with an error message. 

### Describe the results you expected

command returns list of secrets formatted according to the --format flag.

### Additional information

comments.body: I checked the implementation, and the `SecretListReport` struct doesn't contain fields that are present in the documentation. There are two ways to fix this. The simplest way is to update the documentation. The second is to extend the `SecretListReport` structure with the new fields.
